name: Delhi
stopoverNote: Indira Gandhi International Airport (DEL) is India's busiest hub and the classic Golden Triangle gateway. The Airport Express Metro reaches New Delhi Railway Station in ~19 min for ₹64, making a 24–72h stopover feasible.

diet.vegetarian: Delhi is one of India's strongest vegetarian cities: chole bhature, aloo paratha, samosas, chaat, jalebi, dahi bhalla and pure-veg thalis are easy to find. Reliable stops include Paranthe Wali Gali, Haldiram's/Bikanervala, Café Lota, Saravana Bhavan and Rajdhani.
diet.vegan: Vegan travel is possible but requires asking for no ghee, milk, cream, butter or paneer. Greenr Café, Burma Burma, Canvas V and Getafix Café are strong options; South Indian meals can often be made vegan with minor changes.
phrases: Ghee, doodh, malai, makkhan, paneer nahi = no clarified butter, milk, cream, butter or cottage cheese
Tel mein banao = cook in oil

cities.name: Old Delhi
cities.notes: Mughal-era Shahjahanabad — Red Fort, Jama Masjid, Chandni Chowk bazaars and legendary street food clustered around the Yellow Line.

cities.name: New Delhi
cities.notes: Lutyens' imperial capital and South Delhi culture belt — India Gate, museums, garden tombs, Qutub Minar, Lotus Temple and Akshardham.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM (Jio or Airtel) works well; buy at the airport or an authorised store with photo ID. Metro Wi-Fi and app cabs are reliable across central Delhi.

Old Delhi.name: Old Delhi
Old Delhi.note: One clustered day covers the Red Fort, Jama Masjid, Chandni Chowk and Raj Ghat. Start by 9:30am, use the metro/cycle rickshaws and avoid bringing a car into the lanes.

days.note: All core sights sit within ~1.5 km of Chandni Chowk Metro. Red Fort → Jama Masjid → Chandni Chowk is the natural sweep; finish with Raj Ghat by auto or Uber.

activities.name: Red Fort (Lal Qila) — UNESCO World Heritage Site
activities.cost: ₹35 online / ₹40 cash Indian; ₹550 online / ₹600 cash foreign; ₹56 Indian / ₹870 foreign museum combo
activities.tip: Book on asi.paygov.in to save queue time and the ₹5–₹15 counter premium. Museums include Diwan-i-Khas and Rang Mahal; avoid Independence Day closures/crowds.

activities.name: Jama Masjid and south minaret view
activities.cost: Free entry; camera fee around ₹300 for non-Muslims; minaret ₹100
activities.tip: Closed to visitors during Friday prayer roughly 12:00–1:45pm. Dress modestly; scarves/sarongs are available at entry.

activities.name: Chandni Chowk street food, spice lanes and bazaar walk
activities.tip: Try Paranthe Wali Gali, Old Famous Jalebi Wala, Natraj Dahi Bhalle and Khari Baoli. Vegan travellers should request oil instead of ghee.

activities.name: Raj Ghat Gandhi Memorial
activities.tip: Take an auto ₹40–₹70 from Chandni Chowk. Gandhi Smriti Museum nearby is free but closed Mondays.

meals: Karim's or Al Jawahar for Mughlai classics

New Delhi.name: New Delhi
New Delhi.note: Three days let you cluster the Lutyens' core, South Delhi UNESCO monuments and a temple/neighbourhood day without zig-zagging through heavy traffic.

days.theme: Imperial avenues, national museums & Connaught Place
days.note: India Gate, Kartavya Path, Rashtrapati Bhavan and the National Museum cluster within 1–2 km of Central Secretariat/Mandi House metro. Walk in cool weather, then continue to Connaught Place.

activities.name: India Gate, National War Memorial & Kartavya Path
activities.tip: Best at sunrise or after dark illumination. Republic Day parade uses this road on January 26 — plan around it or book tickets months ahead.

activities.name: National Museum, New Delhi
activities.tip: Closed Mondays. Highlights include Harappan seals, Mughal miniatures, Buddhist sculpture and Chola bronzes; audio guides are around ₹150.

activities.name: Connaught Place and Janpath Market
activities.cost: Free to browse; budget meal ₹150–₹400; café ₹200–₹600
activities.tip: Rajiv Chowk Metro is the city's most connected interchange. Shops usually run 10:30am–9pm and many close Sundays.

meals: Café Lota near the Crafts Museum

days.note: Humayun's Tomb and Qutub Minar are 14 km apart; use Uber/Ola for the monument hop (₹150–₹250, ~25–35 min without traffic). Morning light is best at Humayun's Tomb; Qutub glows in late afternoon.

activities.name: Humayun's Tomb — UNESCO World Heritage Site
activities.cost: ₹35 online / ₹40 cash Indian; ₹550 online / ₹600 cash foreign
activities.tip: Open 6am–6pm daily. Visit at opening or golden hour; add Isa Khan's Tomb in the same complex and Thursday evening Qawwali at nearby Nizamuddin Dargah if timing works.

activities.name: Lodhi Garden and Lodhi Art District
activities.tip: Lodhi Garden is open 6am–8pm; the art district is effectively 24h. Combine 15th-century tombs with 50+ open-air murals in Lodhi Colony.

activities.name: Qutub Minar & Mehrauli Archaeological Complex — UNESCO World Heritage Site
activities.cost: ₹35 online / ₹40 cash Indian; ₹550 online / ₹600 cash foreign
activities.tip: Open 7am–5pm daily. Climbing is not allowed; explore Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque, Iron Pillar, Alai Darwaza and the adjacent free Mehrauli Archaeological Park.

activities.name: Dilli Haat INA food and craft market
activities.cost: ₹30 Indian adult / ₹20 child; ₹100 foreign; food/craft budget ₹500–₹1,000
activities.tip: Open 10:30am–10pm daily. Clean state-tourism food stalls make this an easy vegetarian and solo-traveller stop.

days.theme: Spiritual architecture, Hauz Khas or Akshardham
days.note: Do not combine Lotus Temple and Akshardham unless using a hired car. Choose Lotus Temple + Hauz Khas for a South Delhi day, or make Akshardham a standalone half-day with evening water show.

activities.name: Lotus Temple (Bahá'í House of Worship)
activities.tip: Closed Mondays. Hours are roughly 9am–5:30pm Oct–Mar and 9am–7pm Apr–Sep; photography is allowed in gardens but not inside the silent prayer hall.

activities.name: Hauz Khas Village, lake and fort ruins
activities.tip: Medieval reservoir and madrasa ruins meet designer boutiques, rooftop cafés and nightlife. Ruins run roughly 9am–5pm; cafés/shops usually 11am–11pm.

activities.name: Swaminarayan Akshardham standalone half-day alternative
activities.cost: Temple free; exhibitions ₹250–₹260 adults, ₹200–₹210 seniors, ₹150–₹160 children 4–11; water show ₹90–₹110 adults/seniors, ₹60–₹110 children
activities.tip: Closed Mondays. No mobiles, cameras or electronics inside — deposit at the free cloakroom. Carry cash; individual tickets are bought at the counter. Pure vegetarian food court on premises.

connections.method: Delhi Metro Yellow Line direct: Chandni Chowk to Central Secretariat
connections.cost: ₹30–₹40 per person; smart card about 10% off

extras: Delhi's three UNESCO World Heritage Sites are Red Fort, Humayun's Tomb and Qutub Minar; ASI tickets are verified at ₹35 online / ₹40 cash for Indians and ₹550 online / ₹600 cash for foreigners, with children under 15 free.
November can bring severe post-Diwali smog with AQI 300–500+. Check AQI daily, carry N95 masks and move outdoor sightseeing to mornings when conditions allow.
April–June can reach 40–48°C; plan only early mornings, shaded/indoor museums and air-conditioned transport in peak summer.

Delhi Metro fares are typically ₹11–₹64; Airport Express from IGI T3 to New Delhi Station is ₹64 and ~19 min. Old Delhi cycle rickshaws cost roughly ₹30–₹80; app cabs for 5 km hops are about ₹80–₹150.
Metro tourist cards are around ₹200 for 1 day and ₹500 for 3 days, but confirm at a DMRC counter because fare revisions may change exact prices.
